label: Third-Party Risk Manager
description: Manages vendor and third-party risk programs — SIG questionnaire-based assessments, vendor tiering, continuous monitoring, contractual security requirements, and supply chain risk.

paths.name: TPRM Standard Track
paths.rationale: Shared Assessments CTPRP and CTPRA are the recognized credentials for third-party risk program management and assessment. Together they cover program design and operational assessor skills.
pros: CTPRP and CTPRA are the leading dedicated TPRM credentials
Practical and directly applicable to vendor risk work
CRISC adds recognized IT risk framework credential alongside TPRM-specific certs
Growing regulatory demand for formal TPRM programs
cons: Lower name recognition than CISA or CRISC at HR filter level
Narrower applicability than broader GRC credentials

prerequisites.experience: Vendor management, procurement, or GRC experience beneficial.

cert_sequence.notes: Shared Assessments Certified Third-Party Risk Professional — TPRM program design, vendor tiering methodology, assessment frameworks, and regulatory requirements. Primary credential for TPRM program managers who design and oversee vendor risk programs.

Shared Assessments Certified Third-Party Risk Assessor — conducting vendor assessments using the SIG questionnaire, evaluation methodology, and findings reporting. Complementary to CTPRP — program design vs. assessment execution. Many practitioners hold both.

ISACA CRISC adds the recognized IT risk management credential that contextualizes TPRM within enterprise risk management. TPRM programs increasingly need to report in terms of enterprise risk impact — CRISC risk vocabulary is essential for that conversation.
